q: What is the largest land animal?
exp: An African elephant can weigh over 6 tonnes.

q: How many legs does a spider have?
exp: Spiders have 8 legs, so they are not insects (which have 6).

q: What is the largest animal on the planet?
exp: The blue whale can reach 30 metres and 180 tonnes.

q: What does the giant panda mainly eat?
exp: A panda can eat up to 40 kg of bamboo a day.

q: Which animal is known as the 'king of the jungle'?
exp: Lions actually live in the savanna, not the jungle.

q: Which of these animals is a mammal?
exp: Dolphins breathe air and feed their young milk.

q: Which animal changes colour to camouflage?
exp: Chameleons also change colour with mood and temperature.

q: What is the tallest animal in the world?
exp: A giraffe can reach 5.5 m in height.

q: What do bees make from nectar?
exp: A single hive can hold more than 50,000 bees.

q: Which bird cannot fly but runs very fast?
exp: The ostrich runs over 70 km/h and is the largest bird in the world.

q: Which animal carries its young in a pouch?
exp: The kangaroo is a marsupial: the young grow in the pouch.

q: How many wings does a butterfly have?
exp: Butterflies have two pairs of wings covered in tiny scales.
